ask: Let people snooze a saved job instead of deleting it.

story.title: Saved Jobs – Snooze a Saved Job
story.story: As a job seeker, I want to snooze a saved job for a chosen duration, so that it is temporarily hidden from my History page without being permanently removed.

acs.name: Snooze Action Available on Saved Job
acs.given: a job seeker is viewing the History page with at least one saved job
acs.when: the job seeker opens the action menu for a saved job
thens: A 'Snooze' option will be visible alongside any existing actions.
The 'Snooze' option will not replace or remove the existing unsave action.

acs.name: Snooze Duration Selection
acs.given: a job seeker has selected 'Snooze' on a saved job
thens: The picker will offer at least three duration options: 1 week, 2 weeks, and 1 month.
The picker will display the calculated wake date for each option (e.g., 'Until May 28').
A confirm action will be required before the snooze is applied.

acs.name: Snoozed Job Hidden from History Page
acs.given: a job seeker has confirmed a snooze duration for a saved job
acs.when: the History page is loaded or refreshed
thens: The snoozed job will not appear in the default History page listing.
The job's record in saved_jobs will be retained with a snoozed_until timestamp.
A passive indicator (e.g., 'X jobs snoozed') will be visible on the History page.

acs.name: Snoozed Job Automatically Resurfaces
acs.given: a saved job has an active snooze with a snoozed_until date
acs.when: the current date is equal to or past the snoozed_until date
thens: The job will reappear in the History page listing sorted by its original saved_at date.
The snoozed_until value will be cleared or nulled on the record.
No other fields on the saved_jobs record will be modified.

acs.name: User Can View and Cancel an Active Snooze
acs.given: a job seeker is viewing the History page and has toggled the snoozed jobs indicator
acs.when: the job seeker opens the action menu for a snoozed job
thens: The remaining snooze duration and wake date will be displayed.
Selecting 'Unsnooze' will immediately restore the job to the default History page listing.

acs.name: Snooze Applied to a Job That Is Later Unsaved
acs.given: a job seeker has an active snooze on a saved job
acs.when: the job seeker unsaves that job via any surface
thens: The job will be removed from saved_jobs entirely, including the snoozed_until value.
The job will not resurface on the History page when the snooze duration expires.

acs.name: Duplicate Snooze Attempt
acs.given: a job seeker has an active snooze on a saved job
acs.when: the job seeker selects 'Snooze' on the same job from the snoozed jobs view
thens: The picker will display the current wake date pre-populated.
Confirming a new duration will overwrite the existing snoozed_until value.
Only one active snooze per saved job will exist at any time.

assumptions_carried: A1 — The saved_jobs table will be migrated to add a nullable snoozed_until timestamp column; schema migration is in scope for this story.
A2 — Snooze expiry is evaluated at page-load time on the client or via a lightweight server-side filter query; no background job or cron is required for the MVP slice.
A3 — No push or email notification is sent when a snooze expires, consistent with C4 (no notification infrastructure wired) and to keep scope narrow.
A4 — The 'snoozed jobs' view on the History page is a simple toggle/filter, not a separate route; navigation design is left to the implementer.
A5 — Custom snooze duration (free date picker) is out of scope for this story and may be addressed in a follow-on.
A6 — The weekly email digest parked in the icebox (C6) could surface snooze wake events in future but is explicitly excluded here.

clarity.reasoning: The generated story uses a named persona (job seeker), a single-action 'I want' statement, and plain product language throughout. The title matches the reference exactly. The purpose clause is clear and unambiguous.

testability.reasoning: Every AC follows a strict GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure with observable, future-tense outcomes. The generated story actually exceeds the reference by adding AC2 (duration picker with wake date display), AC5 (view/cancel snooze), and ACE2 (duplicate snooze attempt), all with concrete, verifiable thens. Edge cases are well-covered.

scope_fidelity.reasoning: The core scope is faithfully preserved. The generated story adds some implementation detail (snoozed_until timestamp, sorted by saved_at, passive indicator count) that edges toward technical spec rather than product requirements, and introduces a 'duplicate snooze' edge case not in the reference. These are reasonable extensions but slightly exceed the minimal ask. No harmful scope inflation.

completeness.reasoning: The generated story covers all reference ACs (snooze applied, snooze expires, job closed while snoozed is implicitly missing but compensated by additional edge cases), plus adds unsave-while-snoozed (matching ACE2 reference), duplicate snooze, and view/cancel. Six well-reasoned assumptions are surfaced including schema migration, notification exclusion, and custom duration out-of-scope. The reference's job-closes-while-snoozed edge case (ACE1 in reference) is the only notable gap.

grounding.reasoning: Assumptions are clearly labeled A1-A6 and tied to context (C4, C6 references suggest a product context document). Implementation details like 'saved_jobs table' and 'snoozed_until' are flagged as assumptions rather than stated as facts. The reference to 'History page' as the surface is specific and consistent. Minor concern: C4/C6 references are cited without being defined in the story itself, which could confuse readers without that context.
